brief_justification: Arrives at the correct answer (Yes) with valid dual-pathway reasoning; multi-level analysis tracing two compounding negative pathways with short/long-term distinction and even/odd trophic level insight; reasoning proceeds linearly with no explicit reflection on method or bias-checking, only implicit structure in the table format; no counterfactual tests, boundary conditions, or alternative-angle derivations are applied; presents compounding conclusion with high confidence and no acknowledgment of assumptions (e.g., proportional fox reduction, absence of carrying capacity limits on mice); briefly contrasts with simple cascade model but does not evaluate competing scenarios like preferential predation or density-dependent mouse limits; logical chain is traceable via the table and prose but lacks quantitative grounding or derivation from population parameters.
